message:
feat(net): split QUIC accept into incoming+handshake, typed KeepaliveConfig enum, loss detection variable clarity, WebSocket pong batch flushing

Four net-layer improvements from concurrent agent work:

1. **QUIC incoming handshake split (quic/endpoint.rs, quic/mod.rs,
   net/mod.rs)**: QuicEndpoint::accept() now returns a QuicIncoming
   handle representing a connection that has not yet completed TLS,
   instead of blocking on the handshake inline.  Callers should spawn
   a task to call QuicIncoming::handshake() so the endpoint can keep
   accepting other connections concurrently.  QuicIncoming re-exported
   from quic::mod and net::mod.

2. **Typed KeepaliveConfig enum (tcp/stream.rs)**: Replaces the
   previous Option<Option<Duration>> double-option pattern with a
   clear three-variant enum: Default (defer to OS), Disabled
   (explicit off), and Enabled(Duration).  The builder, connect path,
   and tests are updated to use the new type.

3. **QUIC loss detection variable naming (quic_native/transport.rs)**:
   Renames the inner largest_acked variable to local_largest_acked to
   distinguish it from the outer stored largest_acked, and threads
   global_largest_acked through the packet-threshold and
   time-threshold loss checks for clarity.

4. **WebSocket pong batch flushing (websocket/client.rs,
   websocket/split.rs)**: Adds a pending_pong_flush flag so that
   queued pongs are flushed in a single write rather than one flush
   per pong frame.  The flag is propagated through the split
   reassembly path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -433,6 +433,7 @@ where
             assembler: shared.assembler,
             protocol: shared.protocol,
             pending_pongs: shared.pending_pongs,
+            pending_pong_flush: shared.pending_pong_flush,
             entropy: shared.entropy,
         })
     }
